notes.note: Inter scyphos: 'amid the wine-cups,' i.e. at a drinking-party the night before. Cicero teases that even tipsy and home late he could still settle the legal point.
Actio furti: the civil suit for theft in Roman law. The disputed point is whether an heir may bring it for a theft committed against the estate before the inheritance fell to him.

notes.anchor: Sex. Aelius, M'. Manilius, and M. Brutus
notes.note: Three foundational Republican jurists (2nd c. BC) whose authority Cicero cites to refute Trebatius: Sex. Aelius Paetus (author of the Tripertita), M'. Manilius (cos. 149), and M. Junius Brutus the jurisconsult.

notes.anchor: I side with Scaevola and Testa
notes.note: The joke: Cicero ranks his young friend Trebatius ('Testa') alongside Q. Mucius Scaevola, the greatest of Roman jurists, as if they were co-equal authorities, while pointedly siding against the trio of old masters just cited.
